channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: How to Watch NASA’s Artemis II Moon Launch Online - The New York Times
item.description:
1. How to Watch NASA’s Artemis II Moon Launch Online  The New York Times
  2. NASA's Artemis II astronauts are launching to the moon today. Watch it here  NPR
  3. Live updates: Today's South Florida News  NBC 6 South Florida
  4. Artemis II rocket launch may be visible outside of Florida. See map  Florida Today
  5. Artemis II’s toilet is a moon mission milestone  scientificamerican.com

item.title: Starlink satellite breaks apart into "tens of objects"; SpaceX confirms "anomaly" - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Starlink satellite breaks apart into "tens of objects"; SpaceX confirms "anomaly"  Ars Technica
  2. Satellite spies SpaceX Starlink before it breaks apart | Space photo of the day for April 1, 2026  Space
  3. Starlink satellite experiences on-orbit anomaly, SpaceX confirms  news.cgtn.com
  4. Starlink sprays debris into orbit following another satellite 'anomaly'  theregister.com
  5. SpaceX Starlink satellite suffers mysterious ‘anomaly’ in orbit  scientificamerican.com

item.title: April’s full pink moon may coincide with a historic launch attempt - CNN
item.description:
1. April’s full pink moon may coincide with a historic launch attempt  CNN

item.title: Scientists Found a Big Problem With How We Measure Microplastics - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Found a Big Problem With How We Measure Microplastics  ScienceAlert
  2. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics in the lab  Phys.org
  3. Doubts cast on microplastic studies as experts float lab glove contamination theory  The Independent

item.title: Artemis 2 astronauts reveal adorable zero-g indicator 'Rise' photo of the day for March 31, 2026 - Space
item.description:
1. Artemis 2 astronauts reveal adorable zero-g indicator 'Rise' photo of the day for March 31, 2026  Space
  2. Why space crews bring a plush toy to orbit  WRAL
  3. NASA's multi-billion-dollar tech mission still relies on a floating plush toy to signal zero gravity  supercarblondie.com
  4. Meet Moon Mascot 'Rise': Artemis II's zero-g indicator  WESH
  5. Mountain View 2nd-grader designs 'zero gravity indicator' for NASA's next mission to the moon  KTVU

item.title: NASA Just Found Something Strange Inside Asteroid Bennu Sample - SciTechDaily
item.description:
1. NASA Just Found Something Strange Inside Asteroid Bennu Sample  SciTechDaily
  2. Bennu Sample Reveals Three Distinct Organic–Mineral Domains Shaped by Ancient Water  discovermagazine.com
  3. This Asteroid Wasn’t Just a Rock, It Was Once a Salty, Watery World Holding Clues to Life  The Economic Times

item.title: Unexplained sky flashes from the 1950s: Independent analysis supports their existence - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Unexplained sky flashes from the 1950s: Independent analysis supports their existence  Phys.org
  2. Mystery Of Odd Flashes Documented In Sky Before First Ever Satellite Was Launched Gets Even Odder  IFLScience
  3. NASA scientist confirms sightings of 'alien' satellites orbiting Earth  Daily Star
  4. Dozens of mirror-like rotating lights were photographed in the 1950s sky, and the reason their origin matters so much is what they predate  Attack of the Fanboy
  5. Nuclear tests and the night sky  Australian Geographic

item.title: Another 'fireball' spotted in WA. Why NASA says we're seeing more - The Seattle Times
item.description:
1. Another 'fireball' spotted in WA. Why NASA says we're seeing more  The Seattle Times
  2. Fireball spotted over western Washington  king5.com
  3. Caught on camera: Bright fireball reported shooting across the sky in Washington and Oregon  FOX Weather
  4. Two Green 'Fireballs' Streaked Across the West Coast Sky, Some of the Latest in a String of Dazzling Meteors Above the U.S.  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. WATCH: Fireball spotted soaring across WA sky  KIRO 7 News Seattle

item.title: Scientists Just Found a Hidden Critical Point in Water Right Before It Freezes - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Just Found a Hidden Critical Point in Water Right Before It Freezes  ScienceAlert
  2. Scientists Discover Hidden Behavior in Water That Helps Explain Why Ice Floats  Gizmodo
  3. New experiments confirm supercooled water’s critical point  Science News
  4. Scientists claim to have found the two types of water that explain the liquid’s oddness  Chemistry World
  5. This hidden state of water could explain why life exists  ScienceDaily
